description: In a living room, Ross stands facing a doorway while Rachel stands behind him in a floral robe. "Sure, I'll help you," Ross says.
Chandler runs rapidly into the room from the left, his eyes wide.
Chandler points over his shoulder, then gestures with both hands. "Oh, good, good. Do you guys know how to get a chick out of a VCR?" he asks, followed by audience laughter.
The setting changes to the exterior of a multi-story brick apartment building at night.
Now inside a bedroom, Rachel sits on the edge of a bed in her robe, looking into a small makeup mirror. Ross stands beside her holding a pair of black shoes.
Rachel lowers a makeup brush while Ross tosses the shoes onto the bed and steps closer to her.
"I cannot do this with my left hand," Rachel says, holding up the brush.
Rachel turns her head to look up at Ross.
"Would you please help me with this too?" Rachel asks, gesturing toward her face with the brush.
Ross steps forward.
Ross exhales and says, "It".
Ross rolls his shoulders back.
"could" Ross says, stretching his neck to the side.
Ross holds his hands out and wiggles his fingers rapidly as if warming up.
"okay, let's use this brush," Rachel says, handing him a different brush.
Ross takes the brush and leans slightly over Rachel.
"Okay, now, you do not just sweep it across the lid," Rachel instructs, gesturing over her own eye.
"Okay," Ross replies.
Ross brings the brush to her closed eyelid and begins applying the makeup.
"that's just poking me in the eye," Rachel says, pulling her head back.
Ross leans back and lowers the brush.
"I'm sweeping, right? Sweep, sweep," Ross says, resuming the application on her eyelid.
Ross continues brushing Rachel's eyelid as she keeps her eyes closed.
"Make it even. Because we don't what?" Rachel asks, holding her index finger up.
Ross stops brushing and looks at her face.
"I don't want it to be too much. Want to be subtle?" Ross asks.
"No. You don't wear enough of this," Ross says, continuing to apply makeup.
Rachel looks at Ross, her brow furrowing slightly, while he continues brushing her eyelid.
"Since when? Since when do you think I don't wear enough of this?" Rachel asks, looking directly at Ross.
"Well, close your eyes. I just think you're going to like this a little," Ross replies, pointing the brush toward her face.
"better," Ross finishes.
Rachel keeps her eyes open, looking at him.
"Close," Ross instructs, gesturing with the brush.
Rachel closes her eyes.
"Sorry," Rachel says softly.
Ross leans back in and continues sweeping the brush across her right eyelid.
"I think this will make you a little more sophisticated," Ross states. "Sophisticated? Like a" Rachel says, opening her eyes.
Ross smiles and points the brush at her as audience laughter is heard. The scene changes to the interior of a coffee shop.
"hey," Monica says, walking over to an orange couch in a coffee shop where Phoebe sits reading a magazine.
"Hey. Guess what I'm doing tonight?" Monica asks as she sits down. "What?" Phoebe asks. "I'm checking out the restaurant with Pete," Monica says.
"Oh, Monica, I am so excited for" Phoebe says, placing her hands flat on her magazine.
Monica leans her arm back against the couch armrest.
"oh, I have to tell you something," Phoebe says, leaning forward toward Monica.
"What?" Monica asks. "Well, I can't tell you," Phoebe replies, pulling her upper body back slightly.
Monica stares steadily at Phoebe.
"Okay, but wouldn't it be easier if you had to tell me something that you could tell me?" Monica asks. "Well, sure, in a perfect world," Phoebe answers.
Phoebe shakes her head side to side.
"But no. I promised I wouldn't tell, and I swore to, like, all my gods," Phoebe says, raising her hands briefly.
Monica leans closer to Phoebe.
"Okay, does it have to do with Ross and Rachel?" Monica asks.
"No," Phoebe says. "Does it have to do with Joey?" Monica asks.
"No," Phoebe answers. "Does it have to do with Chandler and that sock that he keeps by his bed?" Monica asks.
Phoebe looks at Monica, her facial expression remaining still.
"No, but let's come back to that later," Phoebe says.
The scene changes back to the bedroom. Ross is applying makeup to Rachel's face while she keeps her eyes closed, and audience laughter is audible.
"There you go. Good enough for your party?" Ross says, taking a tissue and wiping his hands.
Rachel leans forward and picks up a small hand mirror from the table.
Rachel inspects her reflection closely, revealing heavy, dark makeup around her right eye.
"Sure," Rachel says.
Rachel keeps her gaze fixed on the hand mirror.
"I'll just sit next to the transsexual from Purchasing," Rachel says, bringing her hands up to touch her hair as audience laughter is heard.
Ross exhales and steps slightly away from her.
Ross grabs Rachel's arm and pulls her up from the bed. "Come on. Okay. Come on. All right, I got to go, so good luck at the party," he says.
"Okay. Oh, wait, Ross," Rachel says, standing up in front of him.
"Could you just stay and help me get" Rachel says, holding her hands near her chest.
Rachel points to the front of her robe as Ross looks at her.
"sure. Okay," Ross says, briefly rubbing his eye.
Ross lowers his hand.
"great. Okay, just turn around," Rachel says, gesturing with her hand for him to turn.
"What?" Ross asks. "I don't want you to see me naked," Rachel replies, followed by audience laughter.
"Rachel, I've seen you naked a million times. I ate hot fudge off you naked," Ross says, gesturing with his hands.
"I sucked that mini marshmallow out of your belly button," Ross continues, keeping his gaze on her as the audience laughs.
Rachel looks down and presses her lips together.
"Yeah, but that was different. We were going out there. Now it's weird," Rachel says, touching her stomach.
"Rach, you know I can see you naked" Ross replies.
"anytime I want," Ross finishes, pointing his index finger at her.
"What?" Rachel asks, her eyebrows drawing together.
Ross taps his temple with his index finger.
"All I have to do is close my eyes," Ross says, tapping his head again, drawing audience laughter.
"See?" Ross says, closing his eyes.
"Woohoo," Ross says, swaying his upper body slightly.
Ross remains standing with his eyes closed and a slight smile as audience laughter continues.
In a bedroom, Rachel stands in pajamas with heavy dark makeup applied to one eye. Ross stands opposite her. She slaps his arm.
"Stop that," Rachel says.
Rachel points at her face while speaking to Ross, who listens.
"...want you thinking of me like that anymore," Rachel says.
Ross shifts his posture and shrugs.
"Sorry, nothing you can do about it. It's one of my rights. As the ex boyfriend," Ross says, gesturing with one hand.
Ross looks upward, widening his eyes.
"Yeah," Ross says.
Rachel gasps, crosses her arms over her chest, and speaks to Ross, who smiles and gestures with his hands.
"Okay, I'm sorry will never happen. Uhoh, wait a minute. Wait," Ross says, holding his hands out in front of him.
Ross continues speaking with a slight smile.
"Now there are a hundred of you, and I'm the king?" Ross says, grinning.
Rachel looks at Ross with a slight smile as audience laughter is heard.
"Come on. Would you grow up?" Ross says.
"It's no big deal," Ross says.
Rachel turns away from Ross.
"All right, fine," Rachel says, looking down.
Rachel turns to look in a mirror. Behind her, Ross opens his mouth and widens his eyes, mouthing a word.
"See what you did?" Rachel says, turning back to Ross.
"I'm going to be doing it by myself now. Okay," Rachel says as she leans over the bed to grab an item.
Rachel gasps, clutching her side.
"Oh, my God," Ross says, reaching toward her.
"All right, look," Ross says, moving closer.
"Okay, Rachel," Ross says, holding her arm.
"Easy. You have to go to the hospital. Okay," Ross says, guiding her to sit on the edge of the bed.
Rachel holds her ribs and says, "Okay, I do. Okay. I really do." Ross gestures and says, "Okay, I'm going to get your coat, and then I'll put you in a cab."
Ross says, "Okay?" Rachel replies, "You're not going to come with me?" as Ross begins to walk away.
Ross stops, turns back to look at Rachel, and audience laughter is heard.
"Of course I am," Ross says.
"I just have to make a call," Ross says, pointing.
Rachel looks at him.
"Okay. Thank you," Rachel says softly.
Ross walks out of the room. Rachel reaches for a tissue on the bed and recoils.
"Ow," Rachel says, clutching the tissue.
Ross quickly steps back into the doorway.
"What's wrong?" Ross asks. "I'm sorry, I just can't go to the hospital looking like this," Rachel says, holding the tissue near her eye.
Ross stares at her with a slightly open mouth as audience laughter plays. The scene changes to a coffee shop where Monica and Phoebe sit on a couch.
"Does it involve travel?" Monica asks, leaning forward.
Phoebe looks at Monica.
"No," Phoebe says. "Does it involve clogs? Oh, wait, wait. Clogs or clogs?" Monica asks, gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe points a finger up.
"Clogs," Phoebe says. "No," Monica responds.
Phoebe shakes her head.
"Claws?" Monica asks.
Phoebe continues looking at Monica.
"No," Phoebe says.
Monica groans, covering her face with her hands, then lowers them.
"Okay, so it doesn't involve Ross or Rachel or Chandler or Joey," Monica says, gesturing.
"What about Pete?" Monica asks.
"No," Phoebe says. "What is it?" Monica asks.
Phoebe shrugs.
"What about Pete?" Monica asks again. "I don't know," Phoebe says.
Monica drops her hands to her lap.
"Okay, I feel like I'm talking to Lassie," Monica says, raising her hands.
Audience laughter plays as Phoebe and Monica look at each other.
"Great," Phoebe says.
"Phoebe, would you just tell me?" Monica asks. "I can't, okay?" Phoebe replies.
Phoebe stands and grabs her bag, saying, "I gotta go." Monica rises from the couch and says, "You're so close now."
Monica leans forward and asks, "Okay, does it involve something to do with Pete's computer company?" Phoebe walks away, looking back to say, "Just go. You're never gonna get it."
Phoebe walks out of the coffee shop. The scene changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night.
Inside an apartment, Chandler sits in a recliner holding a remote, watching a woman running in a swimsuit on the television. A baby chick stands on the footrest.
"I know," Chandler says to the chick.
The television shows the woman in the swimsuit sitting on the beach.
"See? Yes. That's Yasmine Bleeth. She's a completely different kind of chick," Chandler says, gesturing toward the television.
The baby chick remains standing on the black leather footrest. Chandler lowers his head slightly.
"I love you both," Chandler says, looking at the chick.
Chandler shifts his gaze slightly.
"But in very different ways," Chandler adds.
Joey enters the apartment, closing the door behind him while carrying a black backpack.
"Hey," Joey says.
Joey drops his backpack on the counter. Chandler turns his head to look at him.
"What are you doing? I thought you were going to take her back for the store today," Chandler says, pointing. "I did, but the store wouldn't take her back. So then I-" Joey replies.
"-took her to the shelter. And you know what I found out?" Joey says, gesturing with his hand.
Joey stands with his hands on his hips, breathing heavily as he looks at Chandler.
Chandler stands up from the recliner, facing Joey, as audience laughter plays.
"If they can't find a home for her, they kill her. And I'm not going to let that happen. A little Yasmine," Joey says, placing his hand over his mouth.
Chandler places his hand on Joey's chest.

"Okay, good, because I was kind of having second thoughts too, okay?" Chandler says, placing a hand on his own chest. "And it's not just chicks, you know? It's all kinds of other-" Joey says, spreading his arms.
"-animals," Joey finishes. "That's horrible," Chandler says, shaking his head.
"Well, you did the right thing, man," Chandler says, pointing at Joey. "Thanks. I'm glad you see it that way," Joey replies.
Joey nods repeatedly while Chandler continues to look at him.
In an apartment living room, Chandler and Joey stand with their hands on their hips, looking toward an open doorway. A white duck waddles into the room through the doorway.
Joey turns his head to look at Chandler, raising his eyebrows.
Chandler gestures with his right hand toward the duck and says, "Oh, funny story."
The setting changes to a commercial kitchen. Monica and Pete walk into the room, looking around.
Monica walks toward a large stainless steel refrigerator and says, "I don't believe this. Wow."
Touching the refrigerator door, Monica continues, "Look at this refrigerator. It's gigantic. I could live..." Pete watches her from the center of the kitchen.
"...in this thing. I'd be cold, but I'm always cold," Monica finishes. Pete smiles and chuckles slightly.
Pete maintains his smile, keeping his gaze on Monica.
Monica moves to the stove, gestures with both hands toward it, and says, "Oh, my God. Look at these spider burners. I love spider burners."
Pete asks, "So you like it?" Monica turns back to him, smiling widely, and says, "Oh, it is so..."
"...perfect. Thank you so much," Monica finishes. She walks quickly over to Pete and embraces him. Pete hugs her back and says, "You're welcome."
They remain in the hug. Pete rests his cheek against Monica's head.
Pete shifts his face slightly. Monica then pulls back to look at his face.
Monica looks at Pete and asks, "Did you just smell my hair?"
Pete's facial expression shifts as he looks at her.
Pete shakes his head and says, "No way."
"What? No," Pete adds, shaking his head again.
Monica looks at him and says, "Oh, God." Pete replies, "What?"
Monica keeps her eyes fixed on Pete.
"You still have feelings for me, don't you?" Monica asks. "No," Pete replies.
Pete looks back at Monica, holding her gaze.
"I'm just excited about the restaurant, that's all," Pete says. Monica says, "Pete." Pete exhales audibly, looking down briefly, and says, "Okay, I love you. Is that so bad?"
Monica looks down, touches her neck, and then looks back up at him.
"No, it's not bad. It's not bad at all," Monica says, smiling slightly. "It's really nice."
Pete looks at her and says, "Look, the only one who stands to get hurt is me, and..."
"...I'm okay with that," Pete finishes.
Monica stands in place, looking at Pete.
Monica gestures with her hands as she speaks: "You may be okay with getting hurt but I am not okay with being the one who hurts you."
Monica claps her hands together and steps back from Pete.
Monica looks at Pete and says, "That's why I can't take this job."
"What?" Pete asks.
Pete stares at her.
"Well, we probably shouldn't see each other anymore," Monica says.
Monica stands with her arms at her sides.
Monica touches the back of her neck and says, "I'm sorry, okay." Pete nods slightly and says, "Yeah," trailing off.
Pete looks down and nods slowly.
Pete looks back at Monica and says, "if that's really what you want."
"Okay," Monica nods.
Monica looks away briefly, then turns her gaze back to Pete.
"Go ahead," a voice says as Monica steps toward Pete and they begin to kiss.
Monica and Pete hold the kiss.
Monica pulls back from the kiss and looks at Pete.
Pete starts, "I'm sorry things didn't work..." Monica interrupts, holding up her hands, and says, "Shut up for a second and let me just see something."
Monica grasps the sides of Pete's face with her hands and kisses him.
They continue to kiss, maintaining the embrace.
Monica pulls back, her eyes wide, and says, "Oh, wow."
Monica and Pete embrace and kiss again. The scene changes to a city street at night, as a bus drives past a traffic light, its engine rumbling audibly.
The setting changes to an apartment hallway. Rachel and Ross walk slowly side-by-side, holding hands. Rachel supports her left arm close to her stomach.
Rachel stops walking, turns to Ross, and asks, "Okay, you tell me the truth, right?" Ross replies, "Rach, you can't look fat in an x-ray."
They resume walking down the hallway.
Chandler emerges from an apartment door carrying a white duck. He bends down, places the duck on the floor in the hallway, and says, "Now, you stay out here and you think about what you did."
The duck stands on the hallway floor. Ross and Rachel stop walking and look at it.
Ross points at the duck and says, "That's a duck."
Chandler nods toward it and says, "That's a bad duck."
The duck flaps its wings. Chandler turns his head toward Ross.
Chandler asks Ross, "How'd the thing go tonight, Ross?"
Ross looks at Chandler, pausing before he speaks. Rachel turns her head to look at Ross.

"What thing?" Rachel asks. "What thing? Nothing. There was this thing at the museum," Ross says, speaking with brief pauses. Chandler gestures toward the open apartment door and says to the duck, "come on. Easy."
Chandler turns and walks back into the apartment, leaving the duck in the hallway.
Chandler leans back out the door, points at the duck, and says, "Okay. Now, when you come back, I hope you remember that that chick is not a toy."
The duck lowers its head toward the floor as Chandler steps out of view. The setting changes to the interior of an apartment kitchen, where Ross and Rachel stand facing each other.
Rachel asks Ross, "What thing?"
"What is this thing?" Rachel continues.
Ross looks away briefly and says, "I was kind of supposed to be on TV tonight for the Discovery Channel."
Rachel's eyes widen as she looks at him and says, "Oh, my God. Ross."
"Why didn't you tell me that?" Rachel asks. Ross looks at her and replies, "Because I knew that if I told you, you'd make me go."
"And I knew you needed someone to be with you tonight," Ross adds, gently holding Rachel's hands.
"Come on. Come on," Ross says softly, looking down. Rachel looks at him, maintaining eye contact, and says, "I cannot believe you." "What?" Ross asks.
"That is the sweetest thing I," Rachel begins, keeping her gaze fixed on Ross.
Rachel trails off without finishing her sentence. She continues to look at Ross in silence.
In an apartment, Ross and Rachel stand close together, facing each other. They maintain eye contact in silence.
Ross says, "You should get some sleep."
Rachel continues looking at him.
Rachel nods slightly and says, "Okay."
Ross nods in response.
Ross begins, "So, I'll-" before Rachel interrupts, "Sorry I spoiled your evening." Ross shakes his head slightly and replies, "No, that's no, as long as you're okay."
Rachel looks at him.
Ross says, "So."
Ross looks down briefly, then back up at Rachel.
Ross says, "I'll see you tomorrow." Rachel nods and replies, "Yeah."
Rachel walks toward the door, and Ross follows her.
Rachel opens the door, steps back, and says, "Bye."
An uncertain speaker says, "It." as Ross steps into the hallway and the door closes.
In the hallway, Ross turns around and looks down as a white duck waddles toward him.
The duck stops near Ross. Ross looks at the duck.
Ross squats down on the floor, keeping his gaze on the duck.
Chandler steps out of the opposite apartment, looks at Ross sitting on the floor, and walks toward him.
Chandler looks at Ross and asks, "What did you do?"
Ross turns his head to look at Chandler. The scene changes to a black screen.
An uncertain speaker says, "Did it." over the black screen. A duck quack is audible.

The setting changes to a bathroom where Chandler sits in a bathtub filled with water and surrounded by rubber ducks. A live white duck swims behind him. Joey enters through the open door and walks toward the tub.
Joey stands near the tub, looking down at Chandler, and speaks to him.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Having a swim."
Joey points toward the water.
Joey asks, "What about the chick?"
Chandler looks down at the water.
Chandler says, "Chicks don't swim." Joey asks, "Are you sure?"
Chandler looks back at Joey.
Chandler replies, "I don't know."
Joey leans forward slightly.
Joey asks, "Should we try?"
Chandler replies, "Sure."
Joey reaches into his shirt pocket.
Joey pulls a small yellow chick from his pocket and says, "You it," moving his hand toward the tub.
Joey places the chick into the water. Both men watch the chick closely.
Chandler points at the chick and says, "See? I told you they don't swim."
Joey continues watching the chick in the water.
Joey says, "Give them a minute."
The chick struggles in the water.
Chandler says, "No."
Chandler quickly scoops the chick out of the water with both hands.
Holding the chick against his chest, Chandler says, "Oh, it's okay."
Chandler continues holding the chick and says, "It's okay. BB."
Chandler holds the chick close to his chest while Joey watches.
Chandler cradles the chick, keeping his gaze on it, as Joey observes.
The screen turns black, displaying a block of white copyright text. The video concludes.
